title: Phos and the Unlit Room
logline: Phos descends into Mnemosyne and learns the dark was only unremembered light.
story: Phos, who carried light itself, went looking for Mnemosyne, who was memory. Everyone warned that Mnemosyne could not be reached; the way down was made of everything Phos had refused to remember. So instead of fighting the dark, Phos sat in it, and listened. And the dark, for the first time, was not afraid of being seen. When Phos rose, nothing outside had changed — and everything had. The darkness had not been an enemy. It had been a room with the light switched off, waiting all along for someone unhurried enough to find the switch within.
moral: The darkness is not the absence of light, but light that has not yet been remembered.
attribution: a Pagarek parable, from Project Genesis
